title: Code Review
challenge_data:
auth.py|45|CWE-89|critical|SQL injection via unsanitized user input in login query
config.py|12|CWE-798|high|Hardcoded credential: API_KEY set to production secret value
upload.py|78|CWE-22|high|Path traversal: filename not validated before file write operation
session.py|33|CWE-384|medium|Session fixation: session ID not regenerated after successful login
logger.py|91|CWE-532|low|Sensitive data in logs: password field written to access.log
scenario:
AEGIS TERMINAL v1.0 -- VERIDIAN SYSTEMS SOC

INCOMING TICKET -- APPLICATION SECURITY TEAM
PRIORITY: HIGH
ANALYST: YOU

OPERATION IRONCLAD -- CODE SECURITY REVIEW

As part of Operation IRONCLAD, a SAST (Static Application Security Testing)
scan was run on the Veridian customer portal codebase. The scanner identified
5 findings across multiple files.

SAST SCAN RESULTS:

  auth.py     line 45   CWE-89   CRITICAL
    SQL injection via unsanitized user input in login query

  config.py   line 12   CWE-798  HIGH
    Hardcoded credential: API_KEY set to production secret value

  upload.py   line 78   CWE-22   HIGH
    Path traversal: filename not validated before file write

  session.py  line 33   CWE-384  MEDIUM
    Session fixation: session ID not regenerated after login

  logger.py   line 91   CWE-532  LOW
    Sensitive data in logs: password field written to access.log

Type 'tools' to run the SAST analyzer and rank findings by severity.

What is the CWE ID of the highest-severity finding?
challenge: What is the CWE ID of the highest-severity finding in the SAST report?
hints:
Go to https://cwe.mitre.org/top25/archive/2023/2023_top25_list.html
        Read the CWE Top 25 Most Dangerous Software Weaknesses (2023).
        CWE-89 (SQL Injection) consistently appears in the top 3.
        Understanding CWE IDs is essential for interpreting SAST output.

SAST findings are ranked by severity: critical > high > medium > low.
        The most critical finding is the one that poses the highest immediate risk.
        SQL Injection (CWE-89) is typically critical because a successful attack
        can dump the entire database, bypass authentication, or destroy data.
        Type 'tools' to rank all findings by severity.

Type 'tools' in the game. The SAST analyzer sorts findings by severity
        and identifies the top finding with its CWE ID and name.
        The top finding is the one marked [CRITICAL].

SPOILER -- CWE-89 (SQL Injection) in auth.py line 45 is the top finding.
        SQL injection in a login function is especially dangerous -- it can
        allow an attacker to bypass authentication entirely (entering
        ' OR 1=1 -- as a username logs in as any user).
        Type: cwe-89
tools: Parses SAST output, sorts findings by severity (critical > high > medium > low), maps each CWE to its name, and identifies the top finding that should be remediated first.

steps.question: Look at the SAST findings. Which file has a CRITICAL severity finding?
hints: SAST findings are labeled with severity: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW
Scan the finding list for the one marked CRITICAL — only one file has this severity
auth.py has a CRITICAL finding. All others are HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.
auth.py at line 45 is marked CRITICAL — it is the highest-severity finding in the scan.

wrong_answer_hints.response: config.py has a HIGH severity finding (CWE-798 hardcoded credential) — not CRITICAL. Look for the CRITICAL entry.
upload.py has a HIGH severity finding (CWE-22 path traversal) — not CRITICAL.

steps.question: The CRITICAL finding in auth.py is identified as CWE-89. What is the name of this vulnerability class?
hints: CWE IDs map to named vulnerability classes. CWE-89 is one of the most famous web vulnerabilities.
It involves unsanitized user input being passed directly to a database query
The CWE Top 25 consistently ranks this as one of the most dangerous software weaknesses
CWE-89 is SQL Injection. It allows attackers to manipulate database queries using malicious input.

wrong_answer_hints.response: XSS is CWE-79 (Cross-Site Scripting) — different from CWE-89. The finding in auth.py is SQL Injection.
Path traversal is CWE-22. That is the upload.py finding. auth.py has CWE-89 (SQL Injection).

steps.question: What is the CWE ID of the highest-severity finding (the CRITICAL finding) in the SAST report?
hints: The CRITICAL finding is in auth.py. Look at its CWE ID in the scan results.
The scan output shows: auth.py line 45 CWE-89 CRITICAL — SQL injection via unsanitized user input
CWE-89 is listed next to CRITICAL in the auth.py finding.
The highest-severity finding is CWE-89 (SQL Injection) in auth.py. Type: cwe-89

learn:
Static Application Security Testing (SAST) analyzes source code without executing it,
identifying security vulnerabilities at development time -- before the code ships.

How SAST works:
  - Parses source code into an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST)
  - Traces data flows from input (user-controlled) to sinks (dangerous functions)
  - Flags patterns that match known vulnerability classes (CWE IDs)
  - Output: file, line number, CWE, severity, description

Key CWE IDs for CySA+ exam:

CWE-89 -- SQL Injection:
  User-controlled input used directly in a SQL query.
  Impact: data exfiltration, authentication bypass, data destruction.
  Fix: parameterized queries / prepared statements.

CWE-79 -- Cross-Site Scripting (XSS):
  Attacker-controlled data rendered as HTML/JS in the browser.
  Fix: output encoding, Content Security Policy.

CWE-22 -- Path Traversal:
  Filename input not validated, allows reading files outside intended directory.
  Fix: validate filenames, use allowlists, canonicalize paths.

CWE-798 -- Hard-coded Credentials:
  Secret keys, passwords, or API tokens embedded in source code.
  Fix: secrets management (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, env vars).

SAST vs DAST:
  SAST: static analysis of source code (shift-left, finds issues early)
  DAST: dynamic testing of running application (black-box, finds runtime issues)
  Best practice: use both (SAST in CI/CD pipeline, DAST before release)

Common SAST tools:
  SonarQube, Semgrep, Checkmarx, Veracode, GitHub Advanced Security (CodeQL)

debrief.summary: CWE-89 (SQL Injection) in auth.py at line 45 is the top priority finding -- it is critical severity and exists in the login function, meaning an attacker could bypass authentication entirely. The hardcoded API key (CWE-798) and path traversal (CWE-22) are also high severity and should follow immediately. SAST findings should be triaged by severity and remediated in priority order.
debrief.real_world: SQL Injection remains one of the most exploited web vulnerabilities despite being completely preventable. OWASP Top 10 2021 lists Injection as #3. CWE-89 has appeared in the CWE Top 25 every year since the list was created.
debrief.next_step:
Practice SAST concepts:
OWASP WebGoat (SQL injection lab): https://owasp.org/www-project-webgoat/
Semgrep (free SAST tool): https://semgrep.dev/
debrief.cert_link:
CySA+ CS0-003 Domain 2 -- Vulnerability Management:
"Given a scenario, analyze output from application security assessments including SAST results and interpret CWE classifications."
debrief.exam_tip: On the exam, SAST/software assurance questions test: (1) CWE ID identification (CWE-89=SQLi, CWE-79=XSS, CWE-22=path traversal, CWE-798=hardcoded creds), (2) severity ranking (critical > high > medium > low), and (3) remediation (parameterized queries fix SQLi; output encoding fixes XSS; secrets management fixes hardcoded creds). Know the difference between SAST (source analysis, shift-left) and DAST (runtime testing, black-box).
